items.title: James Hughes on AI Consciousness: Buddhism & Posthuman Futures | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

James J. Hughes  is a sociologist, bioethicist, and futurist. He is the executive director of the Institute for Ethics and Emerging Technologies and is the Associate Provost for institutional research, assessment, and planning at University of Massachusetts Boston. He is currently writing a book on secular Buddhism and moral bioenhancement tentatively titled "Cyborg Buddha: Using Neurotechnology to Become Better People".

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

This Chat was made possible by MindFest 2025: "Sentience, Autonomy, and the Future of Human-AI Interaction", a two-day event presented by the Center for the Future Mind.

To learn more about groundbreaking innovation in AI, neuroscience, and the study of consciousness, visit the Center for the Future Mind, view MindFest videos, and subscribe to their newsletter, visit: https://shorturl.at/mYtqz.</article>

items.title: Hubert Dreyfus - Does Philosophy Inform Religion?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

What can philosophy bring to religion? Philosophy is a systematic way of thinking about ideas and concepts, often fundamental features of the world. The philosophy of religion uses philosophical methodologies to examine religion, from the existence and attributes of God to the problems of evil, diverse religions, and conflicting belief systems.

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Hubert Lederer Dreyfus was an American philosopher and a professor of philosophy at the University of California, Berkeley. His main interests included phenomenology, existentialism and the philosophy of both psychology and literature, as well as the philosophical implications of artificial intelligence.

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

items.title: Bernardo Kastrup - Near-Death Experiences and Consciousness Theories
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Many people believe that out-of-the-body experiences (OBEs) and near-death experiences (NDEs) are real. We do not assess the evidence and arguments, pro or con. We do explore what OBEs and NDEs would mean, if real, for theories of consciousness.

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

Bernardo Kastrup is a Dutch philosopher and computer scientist recognized for his contributions to consciousness studies, notably through his formulation of analytic idealism—a variant of metaphysical idealism rooted in the analytic tradition.

items.title: Blaise Agüera y Arcas: What is Intelligence? | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>What is intelligence, and how is AI emergence a natural consequence of evolution? In his book "What Is Intelligence?" and in this conversation, Blaise Agüera y Arcas takes up the idea that prediction is fundamental not only to intelligence and the brain but to life itself, and explores the wide-ranging implications. These include radical new perspectives on the computational properties of living systems, the evolutionary and social origins of intelligence, the relationship between models and reality, entropy and the nature of time, the meaning of free will, the problem of consciousness, and the ethics of machine intelligence.

"What is Intelligence" is available for purchase now: https://amzn.to/3KaJs8f

Blaise Agüera y Arcas is a VP/Fellow at Google, where he is the CTO of Technology & Society, and the founder of Paradigms of Intelligence, an organization dedicated to fundamental AI research. He is the author of Who Are We Now?, and his research has included work on privacy-preserving machine learning, on-device AI, large language models, and human identity.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e</article>

items.title: Is math discovered or invented?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content: <article>We ask Professor Silvia Jonas: Is mathematics invented or discovered? #shorts</article>

items.title: Brian Leftow - Can God Change?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Support the show with a Closer To Truth merch purchase. Shop T-shirts, mugs, notebooks, hoodies, and more: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

If God is perfect, does this mean that God cannot change? Since perfection cannot be improved, would any change diminish God’s perfection? But a changeless God has problems, such as relating to humans in sequence and not progressing. God is more complex than we can imagine, but trying to relate God’s changelessness to God’s perfection probes God’s essence.

Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Brian Leftow is an American philosopher specializing in philosophy of religion, medieval philosophy, and metaphysics. He is the William P. Alston Professor for the Philosophy of Religion at Rutgers University.

items.title: Galen Strawson - Can AI Become Conscious?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

AI consciousness, its possibility or probability, has burst into public debate, eliciting all kinds of issues from AI ethics and rights to AI going rogue and harming humanity. We explore diverse views; we argue that AI consciousness depends on theories of consciousness.

Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://shorturl.at/Xsn2z

Galen John Strawson is a British analytic philosopher and literary critic who works primarily on philosophy of mind, metaphysics, John Locke, David Hume, Immanuel Kant and Friedrich Nietzsche.

items.title: Roger Penrose on Cosmology | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

In the third installment of our six-interview series, hear what Nobel Laureate Roger Penrose thinks about cosmology and his radical view of how our universe began.

Throughout his career, Penrose has challenged conventional wisdom in physics, mathematics, and the philosophy of science. He has tackled some of the most profound questions of the universe: How do quantum mechanics and cosmology shape our understanding of reality? Can human consciousness be fully explained by physical laws, or does it transcend computation? This wide-ranging conversation spans Penrose’s most influential ideas, from black holes and the Big Bang to Gödel’s incompleteness theorems, quantum gravity, and the role of mathematics in uncovering ultimate reality.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

Sir Roger Penrose is an English mathematician, mathematical physicist, philosopher of science, and Nobel Laureate in Physics. He is Emeritus Rouse Ball Professor of Mathematics at the University of Oxford, an emeritus fellow of Wadham College, Oxford, and an honorary fellow of St John's College, Cambridge, and University College London. Penrose has contributed significantly to the mathematical physics of general relativity and cosmology.</article>

items.title: What is Global Philosophy of Religion | Episode 2601 | Closer To Truth
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Global Philosophy of Religion seeks diverse views and fresh ways of thinking from different religions/traditions about Ultimate Questions: God, consciousness, person/self, evil/suffering, afterlife/reincarnation, world to come. It does not avoid hard differences; it celebrates them.

Featuring interviews with Yujin Nagasawa, Swami Sarvapriyananda, Venerable Dr. Yifa, Tao Jiang, Hamza Yusuf, Aaron Segal, Oludamini Ogunnaike, and Helen De Cruz.

items.title: William Lane Craig - Did God Create From Nothing?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Closer To Truth is now on Bluesky! Follow us for updates, announcements, and more: https://shorturl.at/GSR4k

If God is the Creator, did God create the universe out of absolutely nothing? Would this mean that prior to God’s creation there was no matter-energy, no space-time, no laws of physics? Before God brought forth the universe, was there nothing other than God? But if the universe, or multiple universes, always existed, then what need is there for God?

Make a donation to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

William Lane Craig is an American analytic philosopher, Christian apologist, author, and theologian. He is a visiting professor of philosophy at the Talbot School of Theology of Biola University. Until 2024, he was also a professor of philosophy at Houston Christian University.

Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

items.title: Deepak Chopra - Language and Consciousness
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

What’s the relationship between language and consciousness? Which comes first? Does consciousness enable language? Or does language give rise to consciousness? What follows from the relationship between language and consciousness?

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Deepak Chopra is an Indian-born American author, new age guru, and alternative medicine advocate. A prominent figure in the New Age movement, his books and videos have made him one of the best-known and wealthiest figures in alternative medicine.

items.title: William Grassie - Debating God's Existence
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Whether or not God exists is a vital question and that’s why debates about God are important. Arguments for and against the existence of God are productive. Debating God is the best way for clarifying ways of thinking and challenging conventional beliefs on all sides. We encourage debate about God, even when testy.

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

William John Grassie is an American interdisciplinary scholar, social activist, and organizational consultant. He received his doctorate in comparative religion from Temple University and his BA in political science from Middlebury College. A leader in the interdisciplinary and international dialogue between science and religion, Grassie is at work on a book-length study of the evolutionary phase in which human cultural processes begin to dominate the biological development of the planet.

items.title: Chris French on Belief in Paranormal Phenomena | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>How does belief in the paranormal correlate with other religious, political, or intellectual beliefs? What are the psychological tools or categories that can help elucidate why so many people believe in the paranormal?

Chris French's book, "The Science of Weird Shit: Why Our Minds Conjure the Paranormal", is available for purchase now: https://amzn.to/43rxMoQ

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

Chris French is Emeritus Professor and Head of the Anomalistic Psychology Research Unit in the Psychology Department at Goldsmiths, University of London. He is a Fellow of the British Psychological Society and of the Committee for Skeptical Inquiry and a Patron of Humanists UK. He is the coauthor of Anomalistic Psychology: Exploring Paranormal Belief and Experience.</article>

items.title: Markus Gabriel - Realism vs. Anti-Realism
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Support the show with a Closer To Truth merch purchase. Shop T-shirts, mugs, notebooks, hoodies, and more: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

What are anti-realists and why do they deny objective reality? What we know of the world must come through our senses and be processed by our brains. Both can be unreliable; illusions can fool our senses and illness or injury can disrupt our brains. Therefore, can we ever be sure that anything outside ourselves is truly what it seems?

Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Markus Gabriel is a German philosopher and author at the University of Bonn. In addition to his more specialized work, he has also written popular books about philosophical issues.
